reviews.title: Poor management and maintenance of the hotel
reviews.text: My family stayed with the hotel for one night and was shocked by the condition of the hotel for such a well known brand. Wall papers are peeling off with lots of mold clearly visible all of the places, on almost every wall as  in the hallway. Staff at the reception and checkin desk were not polite and courteous. Overall, bad experience, would not stay again and such a contrast to some other Marriot branded hotel I stayed in the past.

reviews.title: Could be improved
reviews.text:
The location is convenient if you’re planning to visit Shanghai Disneyland, as it’s only about a 7-minute drive away. However, there isn’t much around the hotel besides the theme park. Food options nearby are quite limited compared to city areas like Nanjing Road, where there are many restaurants and shops.

The service experience could be improved. The staff were not particularly friendly, and there was no assistance with luggage when we arrived. We had to push our luggage ourselves and walk quite a long distance to reach our room.

The room itself had a few issues. During the colder weather, it felt quite cold as wind seemed to slip through gaps in the windows. The air-conditioning/heater was also quite noisy. Sound insulation isn’t great either you can easily hear people talking or children screaming in the corridor, and even noises from nearby rooms. The wooden flooring is squeaky as well, and the sound woke my baby up a few times when I walked across the room at night to adjust the heater.

The balcony playground area was unfortunately quite dirty and dusty. As for breakfast, the buffet was quite average with a limited selection.

One inconvenience to note is that if you order food delivery, you’ll need to walk all the way to the reception area (sometimes even outside) to collect it.

Overall, the main advantage of this hotel is its proximity to Shanghai Disneyland, but the service, room comfort, and facilities could definitely be improved.
reviews.response: Dear distinguished guest, hello. We are very grateful that you have taken the time to share your stay experience. We are very sorry to learn that our facilities and services did not meet your expectations. For this, we sincerely apologize. We will continue to strive and look forward to your next visit to provide you with a better stay experience.

reviews.title: Mediocre
reviews.text: Unfortunately I cut short my stay from two to one.  Facility is new and there is a sense of calm when you walked in which is good. Check in is long and the staffs lack a smile or even eye contact. Least to say a greeting which I think it’s the very basic. Checking out is the same as well. Only one staff was there at 12.30pm on a Friday afternoon. Left without even a good by from the staff. No sign of a bell hop at the entrance as well. Room is fairly clean as we booked a family room 3066. One of the air condition controller is not working but as it’s winter, we could do with one.  Variety for breakfast if ok, there are kids seating as well which is thoughtful but taste wise, it’s very much to the local. So do not expect more. On the bright side, I was finally greeted a good morning but the staff upon entering the cafe. Wow! Given the location which does not even have a convenience store or anything around, I would take this as a transit hotel for morning Pudong flight, or just to head down to Disney for a stay-over. But with the jacked up price for a four star hotel, I rather stay somewhere in Jinqiao or Chuansha Qube hotel with amenities around and yet get to both airport and Disney easily.

reviews.title: cost-efficient and fun themed rooms!
reviews.text:
We stayed in a camping for two nights and my son really loved it. He calls it "the room with the duckies" and says it is his favorite hotel in Shanghai The themed room was a fun precursor to our Disney escapades and it was also very cost-effective. Service was excellent throughout our stay. We were there during rainy season and they even provided free umbrellas for guests, cheap one-use raincoats, and good-quality rentable strollers with rain covers. The breakfast had a great selection. There was a bus to shuttle guests between the hotel and Disneyland, but the small shuttle during the off-peak times (afternoon) was a little dirty and dingy while the regular shuttle bus was nice and clean. The hotel staff helped with unloading our luggage. 

With these many positives in mind, our stay did have 1 or 2 notable downsides We had to switch rooms because there was a strong sewage smell from the bathroom that became very noticeable once we turned off the AC. This first room also had a huge foot-long crack in the bathroom mirror, a hazard for families with small children. It was surprising to me that nobody noticed this and checked the room out to guests. However, when we asked to switch rooms, the staff were immediately accommodating and kind. Our 2nd room did also have the sewage smell, but not as strong, and we kept the AC on all night to minimize it. Just keep this in mind if you are sensitive to smell. 

All in all, I think this is a great hotel with wonderful staff who kindly welcome families and children.

reviews.title: Perfect for Disneyland BUT far from Bonvoy's Standard
reviews.text:
The room was very clean, extremely clean, and the room amenities were sufficient, as expected for a Bonvoy property.

However, the service experience was disappointing. Most staff barely speak English, and we felt that they were not willing to help.
	1.	I contacted reception regarding lost and found, but the staff simply replied “no, no, no” without checking or offering assistance.
	2.	At check-out time, a staff member rushed toward us and spoke only Chinese. Even after we clearly said “English, please,” she continued speaking Chinese louder and louder, clearly trying to rush us out.
	3.	There was no “thank you” or “welcome.” Staff were on their phones while we were waiting, and when they finally finished the call, they only said “Hello?” with no further service mindset.
	4.	Breakfast had a good variety of food, but juices often ran empty with no refills. There were many guests, yet no staff helping with seating. Staff moved chairs very loudly by lifting them instead of sliding, which was quite disruptive.

I still think this hotel is a convenient option for visiting Disneyland, but the service quality should be much better for a property under the Bonvoy brand.

reviews.title: Choosing accommodation for Shanghai Disneyland is very good.
reviews.text:
I chose this accommodation for my Shanghai trip specifically for the Shanghai Disneyland visit because it has two main points: ① there is a shuttle service from the airport to the hotel and ② there is a shuttle service to Disneyland.

Since I can't speak English, I used a translation software to send a message to the hotel stating that I wanted to use point ①, and I received a reply via email telling me to come at this time.
→ When the time came, a logo-branded wagon arrived.
→ When I told them my name, they seemed to confirm it on a reservation management screen by asking "Is this you?" before I boarded.
That's how it went.

The hotel was very clean, and though I did not meet any staff who spoke Japanese or English, they made an effort to understand what I wanted to ask using gestures and translation software.

In the end, we did not use the shuttle pass between the Disney hotels at the time we wanted, but they were ready to make the bus reservation for our park day right away.

When I expressed that I wanted to go to Disney at a time when there was no shuttle, they helped me navigate the use of Didi and taught me how to arrange a taxi, making me feel reassured about getting on.

By the way, we used taxis between the hotel and Disney at around 8 PM, 10 PM, and 6 AM, and they averaged about 300 yen each. I understand that using the train is cheaper, but since I could get to Disney for just 300 yen, I felt the location was really good for park access. I would like to stay here again when I visit the park next time.

Important notes for park visitors:
- There is no convenience store inside the hotel; the shop is a toy store... you can't buy dinner or breakfast there.
- There are two restaurants, but if you play until the last minute and come back, you won't make it in time.
- The shower can be used both overhead and handheld, with proper water pressure, but there is no bathtub.
- Shampoo, conditioner, and body soap are provided.
- There is a shower room, toilet, and washbasin all in a single continuous area.
- There are no pajamas, but there are adult-sized bathrobes for the number of guests.
- There are multiple direct USB and LAN ports, and as shown in the picture, there are receptacles suited for Japanese standards.
- The TV does not have internet streaming options, and there are no Japanese channels.
- The hotel Wi-Fi is at a level good enough to watch videos.

reviews.text:

Room is comfortable in size and price is reasonable if you don't visit at peak season.
The staff is ever ready to serve and does their best to accommodate to your reasonable needs such as loan of umbrella during rainy season, questions on nearby places to go to, etc.
During our stay, we requested for a late check-out since our flight was in the evening and we were granted the request quite eagerly by the staff on duty, Randolph, Raine & Jerry and feel they should be complimented for their kind consideration, thank you!
If you wish to eat out, then Disney Town is a better option as there is nothing else nearby currently.
Check out the shuttle bus schedules.
Overall, this is highly recommended to go Disneyland in terms of location.

Pros: The hotel and the rooms are very lovely. Very close to Disney and the hotel offers a shuttle. They park the shuttle in the Coach area across the bridge from Disney. Section 9 (though subject to change apparently). Staff is friendly. There is a nice simple gym. The beds are very comfortable and great shower.
Cons: there is nothing around the hotel. The nearest subway stop is about a 10-15min walk and in between no stores or anything really. Disney town is the nearest spot for dinner. Staff doesn't speak much English and there were a few moments if confusion with asking questions about the shuttle and when we asked for an extra robe for the third guest in our room.
Overall great stay.

reviews.title: Exceptional
reviews.text:
We had a very pleasant stay at this hotel. The staff were consistently helpful and polite, and the rooms were spacious, clean, and very comfortable. The room was well-equipped with an iron, hair dryer, and all the necessary shower amenities, which made our stay very convenient.

The staff also went the extra mile by offering helpful recommendations on places to visit and where to shop. Do note that the shuttle bus booking needs to be done in advance, as slots can fill up quickly. There is a self-service laundry room available as well, though it tends to be very popular at night.

The breakfast spread was excellent, with a wide variety of options, and we appreciated that there was a dedicated section for Muslim guests. After checkout, the hotel also allowed us to store our luggage before heading to the airport, which was very helpful.

Special mention goes to Felix Kamar, who truly went above and beyond during our stay. He was exceptionally attentive and even took the time to help clarify our questions when he was off duty. His service made our experience even more memorable.

Highly recommended.

reviews.text:
The room was neither too narrow nor too spacious, and it was hygienic. Personally, I was more satisfied than I would be in a five-star hotel. You can pay about 23,000 won for breakfast, and the food is delicious. 
The reception staff can speak basic English. 
The biggest advantage of this hotel is its proximity to Disneyland, and there is a shuttle bus that runs to Disneyland (the taxi fare is about 1,700 won), making it easy to get around. 
You can be picked up at parking lot 5 at Disneyland. 
The downside is that it is a bit difficult to reach the hotel on foot. If you are coming to Disney, come here.

reviews.title: If it's to go to Shanghai Disneyland, then it's a yes!
reviews.text: I stayed here with the purpose of visiting Shanghai Disneyland! It's close to Disney and has a shuttle bus, which is convenient! However, it's not available in Japanese, so I used a translation device to book the bus! The shuttle bus has a timetable, and you reserve it by providing your room number, number of people, and time at the bus reservation desk near the front desk the day before. There's no need to reserve for the pick-up, you can just get on. There is also a shuttle bus to the airport, which I plan to use! I took a taxi to the hotel. It seems you have to change trains four times, so I recommend taking a taxi. The hotel staff is very nice! The hotel has a gym, a small store, and a 24-hour free coin laundry, so it's great for long stays! They put one milk, one 7up, and one bean snack in the fridge every day! I especially like the bean snacks. It seems there are no other sightseeing spots besides Disney in the area, so I think it’s a great place to stay for Disney purposes!

Special mention goes to Felix Kamar, who truly went above and beyond during our stay. He was exceptionally attentive and even took the time to help clarify our questions when he was off duty. His service made our experience even more memorable.

Highly recommended.

reviews.title: Great stay!
reviews.text:
Superbly positioned for Disneyland and Pudong airport with free shuttles to and from these locations (10mins and 30mins respectively) and other areas such as The Bund. There is a timetable in the lobby and you need to reserve your spot at busier times. A taxi is 20 Yen if you miss the bus and actually drops you closer to the park, which we did a couple of times to save the walk from the bus park and to avoid the hawkers. We also got a taxi back to the hotel from the same area (Lane D at the parking lot). Make sure you have Google Maps (VPN needed)/Amap handy as one of our taxi drivers tried to take us an alternative route to increase the fare.  It's no more than 20 Yen.

We stayed for two nights with a three year old. Our room was a kid friendly room with panda decoration on the wall and panda cuddly toys, a little Wendy house on the patio and a slide. Downstairs there is also a very spacious playroom with soft play and ball pit. This was very much welcomed by our daughter, even after a long day at Disneyland!

The staff are friendly, helpful and welcoming, but speak very little English. Download Google Translate before you visit China. It's a must! 

There's a free laundromat! Very handy.

Unfortunately the breakfast buffet was lacking for me in terms of Western choices and quality, but it will get you by. What was great were the little table and chairs for the kids as they sat and ate together. The restaurant was enjoyable in the evening after a long day at Disneyland. It was quiet and provided ample choice.

The only other niggle was the shuttle bus to the airport. We appreciated the free shuttle on our last day, but the drive was a bit hair raising especially with a small child. The driver drove fast and had to swerve and brake a number of times to avoid other vehicles. This type of driving is common here, but perhaps the hotel could ask the driver to drive more carefully? Seatbelts are provided, so please wear them.

reviews.text: We used this hotel with a 10-month-old baby and two adults. The room was very spacious, and we received water daily, along with juice and snacks in the refrigerator. The downside might be that there is nothing around the hotel, but considering it as accommodation for going to Disney, both the location and price were excellent. Children can stay for free, but there are no towels or other amenities, so it's better to bring your own! There is also a play area for kids within the hotel, making it family-friendly. It seems that stroller rentals are available too. On the first day, we reserved a shuttle bus from the airport to the hotel via email in advance. On the return, we took a bus to the airport, but it turned out to be going to a different airport than we wanted, resulting in us missing our flight. The buses do not have any destination boards, so it's advisable to check carefully!

reviews.title: A great base for Disneyland
reviews.text:
Hotel room is modern, clean and extremely spacious.

We were upgraded to king bed which was 2.5 by 2 m so very comfortably fit the whole family.

There are free airport, CBD and Disneyland shuttles which are super convenient if the times match your schedule.

Otherwise it's only 25 RMB to Disneyland and about 100RMB to Pudong international if you arrive late. 

The only downside is that there is absolutely NOTHING surrounding the hotel so you are only there for Disneyland (which has plenty of food options without entering the park) or a quick stopover in Shanghai if you choose to stay closer to the airport.

We would happily stay again.

reviews.title: Thank you for the staff's amazing support.
reviews.text:
We arrived at the hotel around 11:00 PM. I had to present our passports for check-in, but it was at that moment that I realized my daughter had lost her passport. I thought to myself, "What is this trip? Why did we come here..." I was filled with despair, frustration, and anxiety. In the midst of this despair, a young male hotel staff member diligently helped us using a translation app, maintaining a calm demeanor throughout. He even mentioned that although it was time for him to leave work, he could continue communicating with us via WeChat until we found the passport. He ensured that it was sent to the airport and arranged for it to be delivered to the hotel the next morning. Furthermore, he provided updates on the package's whereabouts until it arrived. 

This person's character truly shone through, and it is clear that the hotel's training is exceptional. I don't think we would have received such support at another hotel. I am filled with gratitude that our major mishap, despite our inability to speak English or Chinese, was resolved quickly and smoothly. Additionally, during breakfast at the buffet, I asked if there was any tea, and I received the response that there wasn't any available. However, shortly after, they brought me a large mug of delicious green tea. I was moved by everyone's warm responses and their thoughtful consideration. I am filled with gratitude. Thank you very much.

reviews.text:
We had a pleasant stay at Courtyard by Marriott Shanghai International Tourism and Resort Zone. Overall, it was a comfortable and enjoyable experience.

All the staff were very nice, friendly, and helpful. Even when there was a language barrier, they made the effort to assist us immediately by using translation tools, which we truly appreciated.

One big plus is the shuttle bus service to Shanghai Disneyland, which starts as early as 5:50 AM—very convenient for guests who want to arrive early at the park.

The hotel also accepts parcels on behalf of guests, which is extremely helpful for travelers who shop online during their stay. In addition, the hotel provides free laundry washer and dryer, which is a great convenience, especially for longer stays.

However, there is room for improvement in how delivered parcels are handled. Parcels should be better organized and sorted by guest name or phone number. Unfortunately, three of our parcels were delivered but could not be traced, which caused some inconvenience.

Despite this issue, we still enjoyed our stay and appreciate the hospitality of the staff. We would love to come back in the future.

reviews.title: The best hotel for Disney purposes!
reviews.text: The exterior looks a bit old, but the inside is very clean! The lobby is also spacious and nice! I arrived at the hotel around 6 AM on an early flight, and they were very kind and offered to hold my luggage for free! On the last day, I could similarly leave my luggage even after checking out. The check-in and check-out process was simple, just showing my passport. They don't understand Japanese at all, but when I said "Japanese," they used a translation device and responded courteously and diligently, so I didn't feel any inconvenience at all. It's close to Disney, about 10 minutes by shuttle bus or taxi. There are plenty of shuttle bus times, and the earliest one is at 5:50, so it's reassuring for early visits. There are also a few shuttle buses to the Bund at good times (around 30 minutes), so sightseeing is possible! I ended up taking a taxi back, but taxis are really cheap. Furthermore, even though it was before check-in time, I was able to check in and enter the room! It's a bit inconvenient that there are no convenience stores nearby, but they were very kind, such as contacting me when my delivery arrived at the room! The hotel was really nice! There are two outlets in the bathroom, one at each bedside for Japanese plugs, and there are USB and USB-C ports on the desk, so I didn't need an adapter. The room itself was very clean, spacious, and had a closet! There was also space to put my luggage, which was great! The shower pressure was fine. I was able to adjust the temperature without any issues! If you're going for Disney, it's an excellent hotel! I want to stay again!
